execution_summary: The Docker image (python:3.10-slim with pandas, numpy, statsmodels) was built and container launched. The Python script replication_analysis.py loaded the de-identified two-wave survey, created composite scores, reshaped to wide form (255 participants with two waves and complete data), and ran OLS Negative_T2 ~ Realistic_T1. The focal coefficient was estimated at b = 2.04 (SE = 0.97, p = .036), confirming a positive significant relationship, though smaller than the original b = 4.02. No major deviations from the preregistered plan occurred; the only code fix was replacing the hard-coded absolute CSV path with a path relative to the script so the file could be found inside the container.

code_executed.logs: Loaded 633 rows → 255 usable participants. OLS: Realistic_T1 coef 2.04 (SE 0.97, p=0.036). R² = 0.017. Results written to /app/data/replication_results.txt.
code_executed.environment: Docker container (python:3.10-slim) with pandas 1.5+, numpy 1.23+, statsmodels 0.13.5

results.hypothesis_tested: Higher perceived COVID-19 realistic threat at Time 1 predicts higher total negative affect one week later at Time 2.

tables.table_description: Full statsmodels summary of Negative_T2 ~ Realistic_T1
